# Agentic Marketing and SaaS Build Workflows

This file connects Claude Code / frontier coding agents back to the original goal: understanding how to ethically lead people toward buying software products.

## The strategic shift

Old digital marketing stack:

**copy → ad → landing page → form → sales/demo → product later.**

AI-agent-enabled stack:

**buyer insight → working demo → proof asset → targeted message → self-serve evaluation → instrumented onboarding → product improvement loop.**

The agent does not replace market understanding. It reduces the time between insight and proof.

## Workflows that matter

### 1. Buyer-problem mining

Inputs:
- sales calls;
- support tickets;
- reviews;
- Reddit/forum posts;
- competitor reviews;
- onboarding drop-off notes;
- cancellation reasons.

Agent work:
- cluster pains;
- identify jobs-to-be-done;
- extract objections;
- map proof required;
- generate experiment backlog.

Claude Code role:
- build import scripts;
- build review-mining dashboard;
- integrate with CRM/support tools;
- create repeatable data pipeline.

Mindset: **mine demand before manufacturing claims.**

### 2. Claim-to-proof system

Every marketing claim should connect to proof.

| Claim type | Evidence needed | AI-agent task |
|---|---|---|
| Saves time | before/after workflow timing | build timer demo, instrument activation |
| Easier setup | setup completion data | add onboarding events and funnel dashboard |
| Reduces errors | error-rate comparison | analyze logs/support tickets |
| Secure/compliant | policy/docs/certifications | build security FAQ assistant with citations |
| Integrates with X | working integration | implement demo integration/sandbox flow |

Mindset: **the ad opens the door; evidence closes the trust gap.**

### 3. Prototype-before-copy workflow

1. State the buyer job.
2. Build the smallest real demo.
3. Observe where users hesitate.
4. Instrument key actions.
5. Write copy based on what the demo actually does.
6. Use ads to route the right buyers into the demo.

Claude Code role:
- generate prototype;
- implement instrumentation;
- create seed data;
- add screenshot/GIF capture tooling;
- build landing page tied to working behavior.

Mindset: **do not sell vapor; compress proof creation.**

### 4. Objection-handling assets

For software buyers, objections are usually:

- “Will it work with my stack?”
- “How long will setup take?”
- “Can I trust the data/security?”
- “Will my team adopt it?”
- “What happens if it breaks?”
- “Can I cancel/export?”
- “Why this instead of competitor X?”

Agent tasks:
- generate comparison pages from verified feature matrix;
- build interactive ROI calculator;
- create onboarding checklist;
- create data-export demo;
- build proof library by buyer segment;
- implement AI FAQ constrained to verified docs.

Mindset: **conversion is risk reduction.**

### 5. Trial activation engineering

AI agents help you build activation faster:

- event tracking;
- lifecycle emails;
- in-app hints;
- sample projects;
- import wizards;
- demo mode;
- onboarding checklists;
- buyer-specific templates;
- activation dashboards.

But the product principle stays the same:

**The buyer pays when the product creates believable progress before attention expires.**

Mindset: **make the first win unavoidable, not the upgrade button louder.**

### 6. AI-search / answer-engine proof surface

As buyers use AI search and answer engines, product evidence must be machine-readable:

- clear docs;
- comparison pages;
- pricing page;
- security page;
- changelog;
- integrations pages;
- schema/structured data where appropriate;
- public case studies;
- precise FAQs;
- sourceable claims.

Claude Code role:
- build docs site;
- add structured content exports;
- maintain feature matrix;
- generate changelog pages from commits;
- build citation-friendly proof assets.

Mindset: **be easy for humans and machines to verify.**

## Where Opus-class agents matter most

Use expensive frontier agents when:

- the work crosses product + code + evidence + UX;
- the codebase is unfamiliar;
- the task has many dependencies;
- the output must be verifiable;
- a failed implementation would waste human focus;
- visual/document understanding matters;
- you need a serious first draft of a system, not a snippet.

Use cheaper tools when:

- generating ad variants;
- classifying reviews;
- rewriting copy;
- extracting fields;
- creating simple test data;
- drafting low-risk docs.

## The final buying-system formula

AI-agent leverage = speed of verified learning.

Not speed of content. Not speed of code. Not speed of hallucinated strategy.

The operator uses AI to produce more verified contact with reality per week.
